# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based Human Resources Management System built with TypeScript and Vite. It follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Core Architecture Patterns

- **Frontend-Only Application**: Pure client-side React app with Supabase as the backend
- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of reusable UI components built on Shadcn/UI
- **Route-Based Pages**: Each major feature has dedicated page components
- **Role-Based Access Control**: Authentication and authorization handled via Supabase Auth with custom role logic
- **Data Fetching**: React Query for server state management with optimistic updates
- **Form Management**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation

### Key Technologies & Libraries

- **React 18**: Modern React with concurrent features
- **TypeScript**: Full type safety throughout the application
- **Vite**: Fast build tool with HMR
- **Supabase**: PostgreSQL database with real-time subscriptions, authentication, and edge functions
- **React Router**: Client-side routing with protected routes
- **Tailwind CSS**: Utility-first styling
- **Shadcn/UI**: High-quality component library built on Radix UI
- **React Query**: Powerful data synchronization for server state
- **Recharts**: Declarative charting library
- **Tiptap**: Rich text editor for HR policies
- **Zod**: Runtime type validation

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Core Application Files
- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point, renders App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing and providers
- `src/index.css`: Global styles and Tailwind imports

### Authentication & Authorization
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control logic
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration

### Layout & Navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout wrapper
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Top navigation bar
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variant support

### Core Business Logic
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave calculation and management utilities
- `src/lib/format-date.ts`: Date formatting utilities
- `src/lib/client-activity.ts`: Activity tracking and categorization
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification system utilities

### Data Layer
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Generated TypeScript types from database schema
- Database migrations in `supabase/migrations/`: Schema definitions and data seeding

## Database Schema Highlights

### Core Entities
- **companies**: Multi-tenant company management
- **employees**: Employee profiles with role assignments
- **projects**: Project management with team assignments
- **clients**: Client relationship management
- **evaluations**: Performance evaluation system (quarterly and daily)
- **payroll_records**: Payroll processing and history
- **leave_requests**: Leave management system
- **invoices**: Client invoicing system
- **hr_policies**: Company policy documentation

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies
- Projects have many employees (many-to-many)
- Evaluations reference employees and evaluators
- Payroll records link to employees and companies
- Leave requests have approval workflows

## Component Architecture

### UI Component Library
The application uses Shadcn/UI components extensively, providing:
- Consistent design system
- Accessibility-first components
- Customizable styling via Tailwind
- TypeScript support

### Page Components
Each major feature has dedicated page components following CRUD patterns:
- List pages (e.g., `Employees.tsx`, `Projects.tsx`)
- Detail pages (e.g., `EmployeeProfile.tsx`, `ProjectDetail.tsx`)
- Form pages (e.g., `EmployeeForm.tsx`, `ProjectForm.tsx`)

### Shared Components
- `SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Reusable employee selection component
- `EvaluationTimeline.tsx`: Timeline view for employee evaluations
- `FinanceSummary.tsx`: Financial dashboard component

## State Management Patterns

### Local State
- React `useState` for component-specific state
- React `useReducer` for complex state transitions

### Server State
- React Query for API data fetching and caching
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Background refetching for data consistency

### Global State
- Context API for authentication state
- Theme provider for dark/light mode

## Routing & Navigation

### Route Structure
- `/`: Root redirect (to dashboard or login)
- `/login`: Authentication page
- `/dashboard`: Main dashboard
- `/employees/*`: Employee management routes
- `/projects/*`: Project management routes
- `/evaluations/*`: Performance evaluation routes
- `/payroll`: Payroll management
- `/finance`: Financial dashboard
- `/leave`: Leave management
- `/settings`: Application settings

### Protected Routes
- `ProtectedRoute` component wraps authenticated routes
- Role-based access control at route level
- Automatic redirects for unauthorized access

## Development Workflow

### Build & Development
- Vite for fast development with HMR
- TypeScript for type checking
- ESLint for code quality
- Vitest for unit testing
- Playwright for E2E testing

### Code Organization
- Feature-based folder structure
- Shared utilities in `lib/` directory
- Type definitions co-located with components
- Consistent import aliases (`@/` for src)

## Performance Considerations

- React Query caching reduces API calls
- Component lazy loading for route-based code splitting
- Optimized bundle size with Vite
- Efficient re-renders with proper dependency arrays
- Supabase real-time subscriptions for live updates

## Security Patterns

- Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies
- JWT-based authentication
- Role-based access control
- Input validation with Zod schemas
- SQL injection prevention via parameterized queries

## Testing Strategy

- Unit tests for utilities and hooks
- Integration tests for components
- E2E tests with Playwright for critical user flows
- Component testing with React Testing Library

This architecture provides a scalable, maintainable foundation for a comprehensive HR management system with modern React patterns and best practices.
